top_signals.title: China’s AI Governance Offensive Threatens U.S. Tech Leadership
top_signals.summary:
China’s diplomats are on an “AI governance” offensive. At a May 5 United Nations meeting, China’s vice minister of science and technology championed China’s role in shaping U.N.-led frameworks that determine how the technology should be built and used. Just a week earlier, two top Chinese AI experts actively involved in Beijing’s governance efforts appeared by video on a Capitol Hill panel discussion hosted by Senator Bernie Sanders, touting China’s contributions to AI safety and cooperation.Norms and standards on AI development and applications are still being defined. Being a standards-setter rather than a standards-follower can simultaneously solidify a country’s technological

The post China’s AI Governance Offensive Threatens U.S. Tech Leadership appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Danish Central Bank Warns of Housing Risks as Price Gains Spread
top_signals.summary: A surge in Copenhagen home prices is spilling over to the rest of Denmark, posing growing risks to the country’s lenders, the central bank said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: ECB signals no need to correct market speculation of June rate hike
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.
